theme: Project scaffold, entity/tile/grid types, a player who can move on a floor, ASCII render loop
summary: Establish the Whisperdeep project's foundation: a Python 3.11+ package that can be installed/run from source, a clean entity/tile/grid model (Tile + Floor/Grid + Entity/Player), an ASCII render loop that paints a floor with the player overlaid as '@', and turn-based 8-directional movement with wall collision. The world for this sprint may be a single hand-built or empty floor with a bordered room — procedural generation is Sprint 2's concern. The deliverable is a launchable binary (`python -m whisperdeep`) that displays a floor, accepts movement input, and respects walls. This sprint establishes the data model, render contract, and movement primitives every later sprint depends on.
assumptions: The project is greenfield; no prior Whisperdeep code exists at the start of the sprint.
Python 3.11+ is available. A virtualenv or simple `python -m` invocation is acceptable; no specific renderer library is mandated by this sprint.
Rendering is to stdout (ASCII). A real curses/tcod TUI is allowed but not required; a frame-string renderer that the CLI prints is sufficient and easier to test.

Glyphs follow the spec legend: '#' wall, '.' floor, '@' player. '+' doors, '<' upstairs, '>' downstairs may be defined as tile kinds even if Sprint 1 does not place them on the map (they become populated in Sprint 2).
A non-interactive / headless test mode is acceptable for criteria verification — most criteria will be unit-tested, not driven through a real TTY.
Pytest is the test runner (matches the rest of the planned project).

criteria.description: A `whisperdeep` Python package exists at the repo root with an `__init__.py` and is importable.
testSteps: Verify `whisperdeep/__init__.py` exists.
Run `python -c "import whisperdeep"` and confirm exit code 0.
criteria.threshold: Package directory and import both succeed.

criteria.description: The package exposes a CLI entrypoint runnable as `python -m whisperdeep` via a `__main__.py` module.
testSteps: Verify `whisperdeep/__main__.py` exists.
Run `python -m whisperdeep --help` (or a `--headless`/`--no-tty` equivalent) and confirm exit code 0.
criteria.threshold: `python -m whisperdeep` is invocable and exits cleanly when given a help/headless flag.

criteria.description: Distinct modules for tiles, the floor/grid, entities, the game loop, and rendering exist (separation of concerns).
testSteps: Confirm each of the following modules exists under `whisperdeep/`: a tile module (e.g. `tiles.py`), a floor/grid module (e.g. `floor.py`), an entity module (e.g. `entity.py`), a game-state module (e.g. `game.py`), and a render module (e.g. `render.py`).
Each module imports without error.
criteria.threshold: All five module roles present and importable. Filenames may vary as long as roles are clearly separated.

criteria.description: A Tile abstraction defines tile kinds (at minimum WALL and FLOOR) with a glyph and a walkable property.
testSteps: Import the tile module.
Construct a wall tile and a floor tile (via constructor, factory, or enum).
Verify the wall has glyph '#' and is not walkable.
Verify the floor has glyph '.' and is walkable.
criteria.threshold: Wall and floor tiles expose correct glyph and walkable flags.

criteria.description: A Floor (or equivalent grid) holds a 2D array of tiles with explicit width and height and bounds-checked tile access.
testSteps: Construct a floor of width=20, height=10.
Verify `floor.width == 20` and `floor.height == 10` (or equivalent accessors).
Read every in-bounds tile via the floor's tile-getter without raising.
Confirm an out-of-bounds query (e.g. via `in_bounds(-1, 0)` or by an exception/None on `get(-1, 0)`) is handled defensively, not silently wrong.
criteria.threshold: Dimensions match; in-bounds access works at all coordinates; out-of-bounds is detectable.

criteria.description: An Entity base class and a Player subclass exist; Player carries (x, y) position and the '@' glyph.
testSteps: Import the entity module.
Construct a Player at (5, 7).
Verify `player.x == 5`, `player.y == 7`, and `player.glyph == '@'`.
criteria.threshold: Player is constructible with position and exposes the '@' glyph.

criteria.description: A Game/run object ties together a floor (or world of floors) and a player and is constructible from a seed.
testSteps: Construct the Game (directly or via a factory) with seed=1.
Verify it exposes the current floor and a player whose position is inside that floor's bounds.
criteria.threshold: Game constructs without error; player spawns in-bounds on the current floor.

criteria.description: A render function converts a Floor (no entities) into a multiline ASCII string of the right dimensions.
testSteps: Construct a floor of width=10, height=5 (any contents).
Call the floor renderer.
Split the result on newlines and verify there are exactly 5 lines and each line is exactly 10 characters wide.
criteria.threshold: Output is exactly height lines of width characters.

criteria.description: A frame renderer overlays the player as '@' on the rendered floor at the player's coordinates.
testSteps: Construct a Game and place / verify the player at a known floor position (px, py).
Render the frame to a string.
Split into rows and verify `frame_rows[py][px] == '@'`.
Verify the count of '@' characters in the frame is exactly 1.
criteria.threshold: Player glyph appears exactly once at the player's position.

criteria.description: Rendered output uses the documented glyph legend for the kinds present in Sprint 1: '#' wall, '.' floor, '@' player. No emoji or stray ANSI control sequences in the headless frame.
testSteps: Render an initial frame in headless mode.
Verify the set of unique characters in the frame (excluding newlines) is a subset of the documented legend ('#', '.', '+', '<', '>', '@').
Verify '#' and '@' are present in the frame (a non-trivial frame must show at least one wall and the player).
criteria.threshold: Frame characters are a subset of the documented legend; '#' and '@' both appear.

criteria.description: The player can move in all 8 directions when the destination tile is walkable.
testSteps: Place the player on an open floor surrounded by walkable tiles.
Issue eight successive moves: N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW (i.e. all (dx,dy) with dx,dy in {-1,0,1} and not both zero).
After each successful move, verify the player's position changed by the corresponding (dx, dy).
criteria.threshold: All 8 directional moves succeed and update the position correctly.

criteria.description: Bumping into a wall ('#') is a no-op: the player's position does not change.
testSteps: Place the player adjacent to a known wall tile.
Capture the player's (x, y).
Issue a move command into the wall.
Verify the player's (x, y) is unchanged.
criteria.threshold: Player position unchanged after wall bump.

criteria.description: Moves into out-of-bounds coordinates are also no-ops and do not raise.
testSteps: Place the player at (0, 0).
Attempt to move (-1, 0) and (0, -1).
Verify the calls return cleanly (no exception) and the player remains at (0, 0).
criteria.threshold: Out-of-bounds moves do not raise and do not change position.

criteria.description: A turn counter (or equivalent tick) increments when the player takes an action.
testSteps: Read the game's turn/tick counter.
Issue at least one valid move and one wall-bump.
Verify the counter advanced by at least 1 after each action (exact increment policy is up to the implementer; >=1 per action is the requirement).
criteria.threshold: Turn counter advances on player actions.

criteria.description: The CLI supports a `--headless` (or equivalent non-interactive) flag that prints a single rendered frame to stdout and exits with code 0, suitable for snapshot testing.
testSteps: Run `python -m whisperdeep --headless` (or equivalent).
Capture stdout.
Verify exit code 0.
Verify stdout contains '@' and at least one '#'.
criteria.threshold: Headless run exits 0 and produces a frame containing the player and walls.

criteria.description: The CLI accepts a `--seed` integer argument that is threaded into game/world construction.
testSteps: Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless` and capture stdout.
Run it again with `--seed 1 --headless`; verify the two outputs are byte-identical.

(Sprint 1 may legitimately have only one possible floor layout, so a seed-1-vs-seed-2 difference is NOT required at this sprint — only that --seed is accepted and that same-seed runs are reproducible.)
criteria.threshold: `--seed` is accepted and same-seed runs are byte-identical in headless mode.

criteria.description: An automated test suite exists under `tests/` and runs via `pytest`.
testSteps: Verify a `tests/` directory exists with at least one `test_*.py` file.
Run `pytest -q` from the repo root.
Verify exit code 0 and at least 8 collected tests.
criteria.threshold: `pytest` runs, collects >=8 tests, and all pass.

criteria.description: Tests cover, at minimum: tile walkability, floor dimensions/access, player movement on open floor, and wall-bump no-op.
testSteps: Inspect test files.
Verify there is at least one test asserting tile walkability (wall vs floor).
Verify there is at least one test asserting the floor's width/height after construction.
Verify there is at least one test asserting the player moves when stepping onto a walkable tile.
Verify there is at least one test asserting the player does NOT move when bumping a wall.
criteria.threshold: All four behavioral areas are explicitly covered by at least one test each.

criteria.description: A README (or `docs/whisperdeep.md`) documents how to run the game, the glyph legend, and the `--seed` / `--headless` flags.
testSteps: Open the README or docs file.
Search for a 'how to run' / 'usage' section that mentions `python -m whisperdeep`.
Search for the glyph legend listing '#', '.', '@' (and optionally '+', '<', '>').
Search for mentions of `--seed` and `--headless`.
criteria.threshold: All three topics (run command, glyph legend, --seed and --headless flags) are documented.

criteria.description: The package has zero runtime dependencies beyond the Python standard library for Sprint 1.
testSteps: Inspect any `pyproject.toml` / `requirements.txt` if present.
Verify no third-party runtime dependencies are declared (test-only deps like `pytest` are allowed).
Confirm `python -m whisperdeep --headless` runs in a clean venv with only `pytest` installed (or with no extra installs at all).
criteria.threshold: No third-party runtime imports; test-only deps are acceptable.

criteria.description: Running the headless CLI does not produce uncaught exceptions or stderr noise on a clean environment.
testSteps: Run `python -m whisperdeep --headless --seed 1` and capture both stdout and stderr.
Verify exit code 0.
Verify stderr is empty (or contains only an explicitly-allowed harmless banner) and contains no Traceback.
criteria.threshold: Exit code 0; no traceback on stderr.

outOfScope: Procedural rooms-and-corridors dungeon generation (Sprint 2). Sprint 1 may use a single empty/static floor with a bordered room.
Doors that actually appear on the map, multi-floor descent, and stair traversal logic (Sprint 2). Sprint 1 may DEFINE door/stairs tile kinds in the data model but is not required to place them.
Field of view, fog of war, line-of-sight (Sprint 3).
Combat, HP, attack, defense, damage log (Sprint 3).
Permadeath, save/load, run summary, daily/seeded runs as a meta-system (Sprint 6 — Sprint 1's `--seed` is purely for reproducibility of the initial frame).
Rebindable keys, help overlay, sound, leaderboards (Sprint 12).
A real curses/tcod TUI is permitted but not required; a stdout frame renderer is sufficient.
deliverables: Python package `whisperdeep/` with `__init__.py` and `__main__.py` runnable as `python -m whisperdeep`.
Modules for tiles, floor/grid, entities, game state, and rendering with clear separation of concerns.
Tile abstraction with at minimum WALL and FLOOR kinds (with door/stairs kinds optionally defined).
Floor/grid abstraction with explicit width, height, and bounds-checked tile access.
Player entity with (x, y) position and '@' glyph; 8-directional movement with wall and out-of-bounds collision.
ASCII frame renderer that prints the floor with the player overlaid.
CLI flags `--seed <int>` and `--headless` (or equivalent non-interactive mode) with seed-reproducible headless frames.
Pytest test suite (>=8 tests) covering tile walkability, floor dimensions, movement, and wall bumps.
README / docs documenting run command, glyph legend, and CLI flags.
